item.description: The U.S. has begun retaliatory strikes after Iran downed an Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz, according to Central Command. "The mission is a proportional response to unjustified Iranian aggression,” CENTCOM wrote in a post on X.

media:content.media:title: U.S. begins retaliatory strikes on Iran after Apache helicopter downed near Strait of Hormuz
media:content.media:description: The U.S. has begun retaliatory strikes after Iran downed an Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz, according to Central Command. "The mission is a proportional response to unjustified Iranian aggression,” CENTCOM wrote in a post on X.

item.title: U.S. begins retaliatory strikes on Iran after Apache helicopter downed near Strait of Hormuz

item.description: All eyes are on a key U.S. Senate race in Maine, where democratic senate hopeful Graham Platner, who has weathered a series of scandals, is likely to win a chance to unseat longtime Maine Senator Susan Collins in the general election. NBC’s Ryan Nobles reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Graham Platner Faces Key Test in Maine Primary
media:content.media:description: All eyes are on a key U.S. Senate race in Maine, where democratic senate hopeful Graham Platner, who has weathered a series of scandals, is likely to win a chance to unseat longtime Maine Senator Susan Collins in the general election. NBC’s Ryan Nobles reports for TODAY.

item.title: Graham Platner Faces Key Test in Maine Primary

item.dc:creator: Ryan J. Reilly
item.description: An election denier who tried to overturn Donald Trump’s 2020 election loss is facing scrutiny over his new role in the Justice Department as part of a team investigating the president’s foes.

media:content.media:text: Attorney Kurt Olsen
media:content.media:description: Attorney Kurt Olsen during his opening statement in Kari Lake's election challenge trial May 17, 2023, in Mesa, Ariz.

item.title: 2020 election denier under scrutiny from fair elections advocates for new role at DOJ

item.dc:creator: Scott Wong
item.description: After weeks of setbacks and delays, Republicans narrowly passed a roughly $70 billion package to fund ICE and Border Patrol through the end of President Donald Trump’s term.

media:content.media:text: Image: ***BESTPIX*** Federal Agents Descend On Minneapolis For Immigration Enforcement Operations
media:content.media:description: Border Patrol agents take a man into custody in Minneapolis on Jan. 11.

item.title: Republicans pass bill to fund ICE and Border Patrol through the end of Trump’s term

item.description: Karmelo Anthony, 19, was found guilty of the 2025 murder of fellow high school student Austin Metcalf at a district-wide meet in Frisco, a Dallas suburb. Anthony’s legal team argued he acted in self-defense after he’d sat in the bleachers under the tent of rival high school Memorial and was confronted by members of its track team and told to leave.

media:content.media:title: Jury reaches guilty verdict in Karmelo Anthony murder trial
media:content.media:description: Karmelo Anthony, 19, was found guilty of the 2025 murder of fellow high school student Austin Metcalf at a district-wide meet in Frisco, a Dallas suburb. Anthony’s legal team argued he acted in self-defense after he’d sat in the bleachers under the tent of rival high school Memorial and was confronted by members of its track team and told to leave.

item.title: Jury reaches guilty verdict in Karmelo Anthony murder trial

item.description: NASA has announced the new crew of astronauts that will take part in the Artemis III mission to test new moon landers. Randy Bresnik, Luca Parmitano, Frank Rubio and Andre Douglas are scheduled to launch into Earth orbit next year to test out at least one of the commercially developed lunar landers expected to carry NASA astronauts to the surface of the moon in 2028.

media:content.media:title: NASA reveals the new Artemis III crew
media:content.media:description: NASA has announced the new crew of astronauts that will take part in the Artemis III mission to test new moon landers. Randy Bresnik, Luca Parmitano, Frank Rubio and Andre Douglas are scheduled to launch into Earth orbit next year to test out at least one of the commercially developed lunar landers expected to carry NASA astronauts to the surface of the moon in 2028.

item.title: NASA reveals the new Artemis III crew

item.description: In just one week, the 2026 FIFA World Cup is coming to North America for the first time in more than 30 years and the United States men’s national team is looking to stretch the limits of homefield advantage. It will be the final World Cup appearance for Portugal’s Cristiano Ronaldo and possibly Lionel Messi of Argentina as well. NBC’s Stephanie Gosk reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Soccer Fever Kicks in Across US Ahead of 2026 FIFA World Cup
media:content.media:description: In just one week, the 2026 FIFA World Cup is coming to North America for the first time in more than 30 years and the United States men’s national team is looking to stretch the limits of homefield advantage. It will be the final World Cup appearance for Portugal’s Cristiano Ronaldo and possibly Lionel Messi of Argentina as well. NBC’s Stephanie Gosk reports for TODAY.

item.title: Soccer Fever Kicks in Across US Ahead of 2026 FIFA World Cup

item.dc:creator: Daniel Arkin
Bella LoBue
item.description: Paramount accused Netflix of launching a “scorched-earth campaign to try and poison regulators and other stakeholders” against its purchase of Warner Bros.

media:content.media:text: The Paramount office building in Los Angeles.
media:content.media:description: The Paramount office building in Los Angeles.

media:content.media:text: Warner Bros. logo.
media:content.media:description: The Paramount office building in Los Angeles.

item.title: Paramount accuses Netflix of ‘scorched-earth’ campaign against Warner Bros. deal

item.dc:creator: Sara G. Miller
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration on Tuesday expanded its list of allowed sunscreen ingredients to include the chemical compound bemotrizinol.

media:content.media:text: A hand holds tone bottle of sunscreen against a blue sky with clouds
media:content.media:description: Bemotrizinol, a UV light filter, has long been available in Europe, Australia and some Asian countries.

item.title: FDA allows popular sunscreen ingredient long used in Europe and Asia

item.dc:creator: Tyler Kingkade
item.description: Google flew 70 teachers and administrators to its California headquarters to learn about its AI products — and how to persuade skeptical colleagues to use them.

media:content.media:text: A split composite image, on left, Winston Roberts speaks on stage, on right, a photo of a laptop screen showing a webpage with text that reads "Google AI Educator Series"
media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

media:content.media:text: An exterior view of Google's headquarters, many trees outside of a building with all glass windows
media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

media:content.media:text: Two overlayed images, on top left, a photo of people looking down at a paper on a table, and bottom right, a hand writing a list on a large sheet of paper that reads "Distracted: gamify learning, pomodoro technique, poll about what is distracting them, activity away from devices, quick time saving demo, address distractions ahead of time (acknowledge challenges), sandbox"
media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

media:content.media:text: Jennie Magiera sits on a chair outside
media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

media:content.media:text: Karen Compton leans against a glass wall
media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

media:content.media:text: Joseph South stands in a tree patch outside of a building
media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

media:content.media:text: Winston Roberts speaks on stage in front of a screen
media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

media:content.media:text: Two overlayed photos, on the top left, a photo of someone look at a laptop screen, on the bottom right, a laptop screen with text that reads "Google Educator AI Series"
media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

media:content.media:text: A hand uses a sharpie to write "Anti-AI, remove fear" in magenta on a piece of large white paper
media:content.media:description: Some educators said they feel it's important to teach students about AI as they're still learning about it themselves.

item.title: Inside Google’s AI training for teachers

item.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

media:content.media:title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
media:content.media:description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

item.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final

item.dc:creator: Tim Stelloh
Lindsay Good
item.description: A couple sustained significant injuries during a black bear attack in California’s Sierra Nevada mountains, authorities said Tuesday

media:content.media:text: An American black bear (Ursus americanus) is walking through.
media:content.media:description: An American black bear in British Columbia, Canada.

item.title: Man fights off bear with hatchet after attack in California mountains

item.dc:creator: Daniella Silva
item.description: A congressional watchdog group found “serious performance and oversight challenges” at the country’s largest ICE detention center, Camp East Montana in Texas.

media:content.media:text: Camp East Montana, an Immigration and Customs Enforcement detention facility at Fort Bliss in El Paso, Texas.
media:content.media:description: Camp East Montana outside El Paso, Texas, has experienced outbreaks of both tuberculosis and measles.

media:content.media:text: Camp East Montana in El Paso, Texas.
media:content.media:description: Camp East Montana outside El Paso, Texas, has experienced outbreaks of both tuberculosis and measles.

item.title: Problems at ICE facility include guard who lost loaded weapon, failure to test for tuberculosis, report says

item.dc:creator: Scott Wong
item.description: After weeks of setbacks and delays, Republicans narrowly passed a roughly $70 billion package to fund ICE and Border Patrol through the end of President Donald Trump’s term.

media:content.media:text: Image: ***BESTPIX*** Federal Agents Descend On Minneapolis For Immigration Enforcement Operations
media:content.media:description: Border Patrol agents take a man into custody in Minneapolis on Jan. 11.

item.title: Republicans pass bill to fund ICE and Border Patrol through the end of Trump’s term

item.dc:creator: Kayla Hayempour
item.description: Sports Illustrated Swimsuit Runway Show models say every body is a target online

media:content.media:text: Ilona Maher, Ella Halikas and Remi Bader walking in Sports Illustrated Swimsuit Runway Show.
media:content.media:description: Ilona Maher; Ella Halikas; Remi Bader.

item.title: Sports Illustrated Swimsuit Runway Show models say every body is a target online

item.dc:creator: Allie Canal
item.description: Kalshi will soon require users to disclose their employer to place certain trades that involve sensitive information.

media:content.media:text: Kalshi App Page Shown On Smartphone With Kalshi Branding In Background
media:content.media:description: The changes at Kalshi come as prediction markets face growing scrutiny over insider trading and market manipulation.

item.title: Kalshi looks to tackle insider trading by asking some users for their employer info

item.dc:creator: The Politics Desk
item.description: Welcome to From the Politics Desk, a daily newsletter that brings you the NBC News Politics team’s latest reporting and analysis from the White House, Capitol Hill and the campaign trail

media:content.media:text: Democratic Candidate Graham Platner Holds A Town Hall
media:content.media:description: Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner speaks to voters at a town hall on June 7, 2026 in Portland, Maine.

item.title: Why Graham Platner’s margin matters in the Maine: From the Politics Desk

item.dc:creator: Doha Madani
item.description: An employee with the Naval Support Activity in Panama City, Florida, was hospitalized following a shark attack on Monday morning.

media:content.media:text: HCA Gulf Coast Hospital in Panama City.
media:content.media:description: HCA Gulf Coast Hospital in Panama City.

item.title: Navy base employee in critical condition after shark attack in Florida

item.dc:creator: Zoya Awky
Corky Siemaszko
item.description: Residents of this ancient and embattled city were being forced Tuesday to choose between two bitter options: stay and risk getting killed by Israeli airstrikes, or leave and become refugees in their own country.

media:content.media:text: A big plume of smoke rises from a building
media:content.media:description: Smoke rises following an Israeli airstrike in Tyre, Lebanon, on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Cars drive on a road outside
media:content.media:description: Smoke rises following an Israeli airstrike in Tyre, Lebanon, on Tuesday.

item.title: Israelis order Tyre residents to evacuate ancient Lebanese city

item.dc:creator: Suzanne Gamboa
Maria Guerrero, NBC Dallas-Fort Worth
Dennis Romero
item.description: McKINNEY, Texas — A Texas jury found Karmelo Anthony guilty Tuesday of the 2025 murder of Austin Metcalf, a fellow high school student at a Dallas-area track meet

media:content.media:text: Karmelo Anthony leaves jail on April 14, 2025.
media:content.media:description: Karmelo Anthony leaves jail on April 14, 2025.

media:content.media:text: Police in Frisco, Texas have charged a 17-year-old student with murder after he fatally stabbed another 17-year-old student
media:content.media:description: A 17-year-old student fatally stabbed another 17-year-old student at a high school track meet.

media:content.media:text: Austin Metcalf.
media:content.media:description: Austin Metcalf.

media:content.media:text: A courtroom sketch shows the district attorney pointing at Karmelo Anthony, center, at the defense table in opening arguments on June 4, in McKinney, Texas.
media:content.media:description: A courtroom sketch shows the district attorney pointing at Karmelo Anthony, center, at the defense table in opening arguments on June 4, in McKinney, Texas.

item.title: Karmelo Anthony found guilty of murder in Texas high school stabbing

item.dc:creator: Rohan Nadkarni
Amina Kilpatrick
Tim Rohan
Greg Rosenstein
item.description: New York City is often said to be the center of the universe, and last night, at least in the sporting world, it was.

media:content.media:text: New York Knicks Fans Gather To Watch Game Three Of The NBA Finals Against San Antonio Spurs
media:content.media:description: New York Knicks fans watch the game on a laptop outside of a watch party at Bryant Park in New York on Monday.

media:content.media:text: A full house at Madison Square Garden during the first quarter of game three of the NBA Finals between the San Antonio Spurs and the New York Knicks on June 8, 2026.
media:content.media:description: New York Knicks fans watch the game on a laptop outside of a watch party at Bryant Park in New York on Monday.

media:content.media:text: Alphonso Davies of Canada controls the ball during a match against Mexico on March 20, 2025 in Inglewood, Calif.
media:content.media:description: New York Knicks fans watch the game on a laptop outside of a watch party at Bryant Park in New York on Monday.

item.title: From The Sports Desk: A raucous evening inside and outside Madison Square Garden

item.dc:creator: Mirna Alsharif
item.description: Decarlos Brown Jr., accused of killing Ukrainian refugee Iryna Zarutska on a North Carolina train, has been found incompetent to stand trial.

media:content.media:text: Iryna Zarutska, 23, was killed on a Charlotte Area Transit System train by Decarlos Brown Jr.
media:content.media:description: Iryna Zarutska, 23, was killed on a Charlotte Area Transit System train by Decarlos Brown Jr.

media:content.media:text: Iryna Zarutska.
media:content.media:description: Iryna Zarutska, 23, was killed on a Charlotte Area Transit System train by Decarlos Brown Jr.

item.title: Man accused of killing Ukrainian woman on North Carolina train found incompetent to stand trial

item.dc:creator: Rebecca Cohen
item.description: A former pilot flew commercial planes for nearly 17 years allegedly without the required license, Ontario police said

media:content.media:text: Air Canada Boeing 777 taking off from Vancouver International Airport
media:content.media:description: Air Canada Boeing 777 taking off from Vancouver International Airport in 2022.

item.title: Former Air Canada pilot flew hundreds of flights without proper license, police say

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
Rebecca Cohen
item.description: The union representing workers at SoFi Stadium, the Los Angeles-area arena hosting the FIFA World Cup, has reached a tentative agreement with operators at the stadium after voting overwhelmingly last week to authorize a strike — and just days before the venue is scheduled to host its first of several matches.

media:content.media:text: SoFi Stadium workers arrive at union headquarters
media:content.media:description: SoFi Stadium workers arrive at union headquarters for a strike vote in Inglewood, Calif., on Thursday.

item.title: Workers at L.A. World Cup stadium reach tentative agreement after authorizing strike

item.dc:creator: Jared Perlo
item.description: Anthropic released its latest model Tuesday afternoon, heralding the public’s first access to the AI company’s most powerful class of AI systems.

media:content.media:text: AI company Anthropic
media:content.media:description: The Anthropic logo as seen at an event organized by the AI company.

item.title: Anthropic releases Fable 5 model, built on the same tech that spooked the government
